global.title: Single-user feeds remain intact as personal backups when a doc becomes collaborative
global.content:
# Single-User Feeds Persist During Collaboration

Collaboration does not rewrite or migrate the single-user feed layout. When Alice shares `doc-001` with Bob:

## Alice still owns and maintains

- `ph:v2:user:0xalice` — user manifest
- `ph:v2:drive:drive-abc` — drive manifest
- `ph:v2:doc:doc-001` — document manifest (her personal backup)

## And adds on top

- `ph:v2:collab:doc-001` — collaboration index
- `ph:v2:ops:0xalice_signer:doc-001` — her op feed

## Bob brings his own

- `ph:v2:user:0xbob` — Bob's user manifest
- `ph:v2:ops:0xbob_signer:doc-001` — Bob's op feed

## Why

- Single-user feeds serve as the user's personal recovery path — they can reconstruct their whole vault independently
- Collaboration adds a real-time transport layer, it doesn't own the source of truth
- If collaboration ends, the single-user feeds continue working unchanged
global.description: When a document becomes collaborative, the original user/drive/doc manifest feeds remain as personal backups; collab feeds layer additively on top rather than replace.
